requirement: Consumer devices with wired charging sold in the EU must use a USB-C port.
gap: USB-C is required for wired charging on this product class.
recommended_action: Replace the wired charging port with USB-C.

alert.message: LumaNord Lighting GmbH: your FlexGlow RGB Strip 5m must comply with common_charger by 2024-12-28. Replace the wired charging port with USB-C. Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32022R2380

requirement: Electronics manufacturers selling in the EU are obligated to notify the European Chemicals Agency (ECHA) under Article 33 of the REACH regulation if their products contain Substances of Very High Concern (SVHCs) above a 0.1% weight-by-weight (w/w) threshold. This notification duty is fulfilled by filing information in the Substances of Concern In articles, as such or in complex objects (Products) (SCIP) database. The current obligation to file in the SCIP database has been in effect since January 5, 2021, for products containing SVHCs on the candidate list, with no specific deadline for compliance other than the requirement to file within six months of the SVHC being added to the candidate list for new additions.
gap: SVHC screening and customer/SCIP duties may apply.
recommended_action: Screen articles and file SCIP notifications where required.

alert.message: LumaNord Lighting GmbH: your FlexGlow RGB Strip 5m must comply with reach_svhc by 2026-10-30. Screen articles and file SCIP notifications where required. Source: https://echa.europa.eu/candidate-list-table

requirement: Plastic packaging placed on the EU market must meet mandatory minimum recycled-content targets. For contact-sensitive plastic packaging (e.g. consumer electronics retail packaging) the initial target is 10 % recycled content by 2030, rising to 50 % by 2040. Producers must declare recycled content on the packaging or a technical file available on request.
gap: 'FlexGlow RGB Strip 5m' may be subject to PPWR-2025-ART7-RECYCLED-CONTENT: Plastic packaging placed on the EU market must meet mandatory minimum recycled-content targets. For contact-sensitive pl
recommended_action: Review Article 7 obligations and ensure compliance before 2030-01-01.

alert.message: LumaNord Lighting GmbH: your FlexGlow RGB Strip 5m must comply with PPWR-2025-ART7-RECYCLED-CONTENT by 2030-01-01. Review Article 7 obligations and ensure compliance before 2030-01-01. Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2025/40/oj

requirement: Portable and button-cell batteries in consumer/toy products must be user-removable.
gap: Battery must be user-removable and replaceable.
recommended_action: Redesign enclosure so the user can remove and replace the battery.

alert.message: CellCore Energy Sp. z o.o.: your PowerBank 20k Portable must comply with battery_removability by 2027-02-18. Redesign enclosure so the user can remove and replace the battery. Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/1542/oj

requirement: LMT and industrial batteries >2 Wh must carry a digital battery passport via QR code.
gap: Battery passport required for LMT or industrial battery > 2 kWh.
recommended_action: Create QR/data-carrier battery passport before the deadline.

alert.message: CellCore Energy Sp. z o.o.: your IndusCell 2.5kWh Module must comply with battery_passport by 2027-02-18. Create QR/data-carrier battery passport before the deadline. Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/1542/oj

requirement: EV and industrial batteries must carry a carbon footprint declaration.
gap: Carbon footprint declaration required for EV or industrial battery.
recommended_action: Calculate and declare lifecycle carbon footprint per Annex II methodology.

requirement: Any manufacturer or authorised representative that places electrical or electronic equipment (EEE) on the German market must register with the stiftung ear (Gemeinsames Rücknahmesystem) before the first placing on the market. Registration must state the equipment category (ElektroG Annex I), brand, and annual quantity placed. Unregistered EEE may not be sold in Germany.

recommended_action: Review § 6 ElektroG (Elektro- und Elektronikgerätegesetz) obligations and ensure compliance before 2024-01-01.

requirement: Portable and button-cell batteries in consumer/toy products must be user-removable.
gap: Battery must be user-removable and replaceable.
recommended_action: Redesign enclosure so the user can remove and replace the battery.

alert.message: GlowToys Junior Sp. z o.o.: your MelodyPlush Toy must comply with battery_removability by 2027-02-18. Redesign enclosure so the user can remove and replace the battery. Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/1542/oj

requirement: Consumer/toy products with button-cell batteries need a tool-required compartment.
gap: Button-cell compartment must be secured against child access.
recommended_action: Add tool-required or screw-fastened battery compartment and warnings.

alert.message: GlowToys Junior Sp. z o.o.: your MelodyPlush Toy must comply with gpsr_button_cell by 2024-12-13. Add tool-required or screw-fastened battery compartment and warnings. Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/988/oj

requirement: Electronics manufacturers selling in the EU are obligated to notify the European Chemicals Agency (ECHA) under Article 33 of the REACH regulation if their products contain Substances of Very High Concern (SVHCs) above a 0.1% weight-by-weight (w/w) threshold. This notification duty is fulfilled by filing information in the Substances of Concern In articles, as such or in complex objects (Products) (SCIP) database. The current obligation to file in the SCIP database has been in effect since January 5, 2021, for products containing SVHCs on the candidate list, with no specific deadline for compliance other than the requirement to file within six months of the SVHC being added to the candidate list for new additions.
gap: SVHC screening and customer/SCIP duties may apply.

gap: Carbon footprint declaration required for EV or industrial battery.
